questions.question: The deinstitutionalization movement of the 1960s aimed to:

options.text: Build larger, more modern psychiatric hospitals
Close psychiatric hospitals and shift care to community-based mental health centers
Eliminate all forms of medication for psychological disorders
Require people with mental illness to receive treatment through the criminal justice system

questions.explanation: Deinstitutionalization aimed to close large state psychiatric hospitals and replace them with community-based care. While the vision was to provide humane, community-integrated treatment, inadequate funding led to many individuals experiencing homelessness or incarceration.

questions.question: Which reformer is credited with campaigning for state-funded hospitals to provide humane care for people with mental illness in the United States?
questions.explanation: Dorothea Dix was a 19th-century American reformer who campaigned tirelessly for the establishment of state-funded hospitals that would provide clean, safe environments with compassionate care for people with psychological disorders.
